items.title: World Cup 2026 : England to face Argentina in tense semi-final
items.description: Switzerland meanwhile is eliminated after losing to Argentina 3-1. Mac Allister gave the reigning champions the lead only ten minutes into the game, heading the ball past Gregor Kobel off a Leo Messi cross. Switzerland gained in momentum, culminating in an equalizer from Dan Ndoye. But that goal was quickly overshadowed after Embolo was handed a second yellow for diving and was sent off. That obviously made the challenge even greater for Switzerland, but la Nati defended valiantly one man down, dragging Argentina into extra time. In the end though, Julian Alvarez scored a stunning goal curled in from outside the box. As Switzerland pushed for a second leveller, they were caught on the counter, and suffered a third and final goal at the hands of an unconvincing Argentinian side...

items.description: With the world population surpassing 8.3 billion today, can we truly realize the hopes and dreams of the largest generation of young people ever? From family planning to sustainable development, this global observance asks a vital question: are we investing enough in tomorrow's leaders today? Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Diene Keita, Executive Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

items.title: IMF confirms 3.5% growth forecast for Argentina in 2026
items.description: Multilateral lender’s chief Kristalina Georgieva to visit Buenos Aires this month; International Monetary Fund sees Argentina’s economy expanding 4% in 2027. Leer más

items.title: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road
items.description: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

items.title: INEC extends deadline for parties to submit candidates
items.description: “The decision to extend was based on an appeal by the Inter-Party Advisory Council, on behalf of political parties who were unable to upload the names and personal particulars of their candidates within the scheduled timeline,” the statement said. The post INEC extends deadline for parties to submit candidates appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: World Cup culture wars and the legend of Lionel Messi
items.description: World Cups don’t occur in a vacuum. Rather, they are an integral part of the construction of cultural subjectivity across the globe given the prevalence and capillarity of football. Argentina’s central role in global football and its obsessed population add complexity and intensity. Leer más

items.title: Spain: Nine injured in fourth San Fermín bull run in Pamplona
items.description: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

items.title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; target ₹35,000 crore trade by 2030
items.description: A joint statement noted that Narendra Modi and Christopher Luxon exchanged views on the Indo-Pacific, reaffirming their shared commitment to a free, open, and prosperous region

items.title: Man wanted over alleged attempted murder of 74yo Sydney man
items.description: An arrest warrant has been issued for a 45-year-old man in connection to a violent assault in Western Sydney on Saturday.

items.title: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia express concern over escalation in  West Asia conflict
items.description: The Pakistani and Saudi Foreign Ministers exchanged views on the recent developments in the region and expressed deep concern over the escalations despite the signing of the Islamabad MoU

items.title: West Asia war updates: Iran warns it will not be bound by deal with U.S. if violations continue
items.description: Iran's supreme leader ​Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said ‌that ​avenging his ⁠slain predecessor and father was "the demand of ‌the nation" and "must certainly" take ‌place, according ‌to ⁠a written ⁠message released on his Telegram account

items.title: Watch: Inside the 30-hour dating retreat run by monks
items.description: Forget coffee or drinks, the latest dating trend in South Korea takes place somewhere very different, a Buddhist temple.

items.title: Milei urges governors to renew commitment to 'May Pact' in speech
items.description: President says Argentina is entering a "Second Independence" as he outlines new reform agenda and praises his "cabinet of patriots." Leer más

items.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
items.description: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death
items.description: The family of Perth man Cameron Hughes, who died in a Bali immigration detention centre, says it has many questions about the circumstances surrounding his death.

items.title: Granit Xhaka tells Swiss fans to 'keep dreaming' ahead of Argentina World Cup clash
items.description: Skipper said Switzerland's "overarching aim" was to beat the defending champions and reach the semi-finals for the first time. Leer más

items.title: The week in pictures: Le Pen's comeback, Khamenei's funeral and Mbappé's wondergoal
items.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was cleared to run in next year's presidential election, despite having her embezzlement conviction confirmed, in a week that saw Iran hold days-long funeral ceremonies for slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and football stars Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi deliver moments of magic to carry their teams into the World Cup semi-finals. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Bellingham's brace sends England past Norway to semi-finals
Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
items.description: Sheikh Hamad, who ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, was a key architect of the energy-rich Gulf country's development.

items.title: Ukraine's Bidnyi 'outraged' by Russia's Olympic return
items.description: In exclusive comments to DW, Ukraine's sports minister, Matviy Bidnyi, said he is outraged by the IOC opening the door to Russia's return to the Olympics

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. items.title: 'Fierce attack': Victim refuses to clean antisemitic graffiti off property
items.description: The victim of an antisemitic attack says he doesn't want to clean up offensive graffiti sprayed on his Adelaide property, because he wants the public to see his family was targeted

items.title: Why did the U.S. indict Lawrence Bishnoi over Hardeep Singh Nijjar’s murder?
items.description: The U.S. has indicted Lawrence Bishnoi, a jailed Indian gangster, for his alleged role in a transnational conspiracy linked to the murder of Khalistani separatist Hardeep Singh Nijjar in Canada in June 2023.

items.title: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei laid to rest in Mashhad
items.description: Thousands of mourners performed funeral prayers Thursday on the final day of ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party
items.description: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

items.description: President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi of Egypt hosted the country's World Cup soccer team a day after their elimination in the round of 16 by Argentina.

items.title: Mojtaba vows revenge for father’s killing after Trump’s new threat
items.description: ‘Vengeance is the will of our nation and must inevitably be carried out,’ says Iran’s Supreme Leader in a written message; earlier Trump had said any attempt to assassinate him would lead the U.S. to ‘completely decimate’ Iran

items.title: ‘La cuarta estrella’: Argentina's players embrace fans' new World Cup anthem
items.description: 'La cuarta estrella' has swept through the stands before being adopted by the players themselves after their dramatic victory over Egypt, rekindling memories of Qatar 2022’s 'Muchachos.' Leer más
